what_it_does: Builds registry/graph.json from registry/address-registry.json by grouping file addresses into top-level-folder clusters, annotating nodes with type/icon/summary/metrics, emitting resolved file-to-file edges, a type legend, spine ids, root metadata, and generation metadata.
observations: Loads address registry JSON, changes working directory to the project root, and writes registry/graph.json directly.
Defines a local TYPEMAP from detected file types/extensions to DNA line-icon names and plain-English labels.
Only file entries become graph nodes; ghost entries are counted in metadata but not emitted as nodes.
Clusters are keyed by the first path segment and contain full-depth file nodes under that top-level folder.
Node fields include refs, bytes, lines, depth, alphabetical order, mtime, summary, spine flag, and outbound resolved edge count.
Edges are deduplicated resolved file-to-file relationships where the target is a real file node.
Spine is derived from file in-degree and limited to addressed files with at least one incoming edge.
The script reports unmapped types that fall back to the default file icon.
standouts: The graph output is a projection of the address registry rather than a separate scanner.
It preserves several projectable dimensions for layout: hierarchy depth, alphabetical order, modification time, byte size, refs, and edge counts.
Type presentation is intentionally human-readable, pairing detected substrate types with icon names and labels.
conventions: Uses small file-local helper functions for type labeling, summaries, kind detection, path grouping, reference counts, and mtimes.
Uses plain dictionaries/lists and json.dump with indent=1 for deterministic structured output shape.
Uses comments to describe rendering intent and geometry semantics alongside data construction.
Performs work at module import/runtime level instead of wrapping execution in a main function.
concerns: The script opens and writes files without context managers.
It changes the process working directory globally at import/runtime.
Missing registry/address-registry.json or malformed expected keys will raise directly with no file-local recovery path.
The root calculation depends on this file remaining exactly three directories below the project root.
mtime(a) assumes address ids are usable filesystem paths from the changed working directory.
notes: This is a registry-to-graph projection script that turns addressed files and typed relationships into the JSON payload expected by a graph renderer.
questions: Are directory entries intentionally excluded from clusters except through their contained file paths?
Should edge kind values be normalized or validated before being written?
Should generated timestamps be UTC-only for all consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it projects the address registry into registry/graph.json as a geometric graph data view with clusters, nodes, edges, types, spine, and metadata.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make an address registry renderable as an interpretable graph by preserving address identity while adding layout-friendly metrics and readable type labels.
novelty: It treats code and design files as typed substrate nodes with iconography, summaries, hierarchy, ordinal position, time, and reference prominence in one emitted graph payload.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete graph data projection layer: it translates addressed file records and resolved relationships into clustered nodes, typed legends, edge lists, spine markers, and render-oriented metrics that a fused system would keep as a substrate visualization feed.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that reads registry/address-registry.json and writes registry/graph.json containing clustered file nodes, resolved file edges, type legend entries, spine ids, root metadata, and per-node layout metrics.
intention_for_embedding: It was for rendering the addressed code/design substrate as a neutral graph view without creating a separate source of record.

symbols.does: Maps a type string to an icon name and plain-English label.
symbols.description: It lowercases the base part before a dot, checks TYPEMAP by that base, then checks the full type, and finally falls back to a generic file icon with the type or "File" label. This keeps graph labels readable while allowing prefixed or compound type values to resolve through their base type.

symbols.does: Extracts a structure summary from a census objective-record attachment.
symbols.description: It scans an entry's attachments and returns structure_summary from the first attachment whose type is census.objective-record.v1. If no matching attachment or summary is present, it returns an empty string.

symbols.does: Determines the graph type string for an address-registry entry.
symbols.description: It maps directory kind to "dir" and ghost kind to "ghost". For other entries it uses type_detected, then extension, then "file" as the fallback.

symbols.does: Returns the first path segment of an address id.
symbols.description: It groups paths by the text before the first slash. Addresses without a slash are assigned to the literal "(root)" group.

symbols.does: Counts incoming typed inverse edges for an entry.
symbols.description: It returns the length of the entry's edges_in list, defaulting to an empty list when absent. The value is used for spine selection, node refs, cluster refs, and member ordering by reference count.

symbols.does: Returns a filesystem modification time for an address path, or null if unavailable.
symbols.description: It calls os.path.getmtime on the address string and casts the result to an integer timestamp. If the path is not readable or missing, it catches OSError and returns None.
